results.seo_title: NVIDIA's Hermes Unlocks Self-Improving AI Agents
results.meta_description: Hermes Agent, powered by NVIDIA RTX PCs and DGX Spark, is a self-improving AI agent that crosses 140,000 GitHub stars in under three months.
results.body_html:
Hermes Agent, powered by NVIDIA RTX PCs and DGX Spark, is a self-improving AI agent that crosses 140,000 GitHub stars in under three months.

Self-Evolving Skills: Hermes writes and refines its own skills every time it encounters a complex task or receives feedback. Contained Sub-Agents: Hermes treats sub-agents as short-lived, isolated workers dedicated to a sub-task with a focused context and set of tools. Reliability by design: Nous Research curates and stress-tests every skill, tool, and plug-in that ships with Hermes.

NVIDIA RTX PCs/DGX SparkAcceleration for Agentic AI

NVIDIA RTX PCs/DGX Spark Accelerated agentic AI with high-performance, open-weight large language models (LLMs)

Qwen 3.6 LLMs for Hermes Qwen 3.6 27B and 35B parameter models outperform previous-generation 120B and 400B parameter model counterparts, running on NVIDIA RTX and DGX Spark.

source.title: Hermes Unlocks Self-Improving AI Agents, Powered by NVIDIA RTX PCs and DGX Spark
source.summary: Agentic AI is changing the way users get work done. Following the success of OpenClaw, the community is embracing new open source agentic frameworks. The latest is Hermes Agent, which crossed 140,000 GitHub stars in under three months.

results.seo_title: Data Readiness for Agentic AI in Financial Services
results.meta_description: Financial services companies need authoritative context data to succeed with agentic AI.
results.body_html:
The success of agentic AI in financial services depends on an authoritative context data store.

Key Requirements for Financial Services DataAccessible and reliable dataGoverned at scaleContextual understandingData Governance ChallengesChallengeDescriptionRegulatory requirementsExigent regulatory requirements can hinder data availability and quality.Lack of standardizationInconsistent data formats and structures can lead to errors and inefficiencies.Data security concernsFinancial services companies must prioritize data security to protect sensitive information.Benefits of Agentic AI in Financial ServicesThe use of agentic AI can improve financial services by providing contextual understanding and automating decision-making processes.

source.title: Data readiness for agentic AI in financial services
source.summary: Financial services companies have unique needs when it comes to business AI. They operate in one of the most highly regulated sectors while responding to external events that are updated by the second. As a result, the success of agentic AI in financial services depends less on the sophistication of the system and more on…

results.seo_title: Robinhood lets AI agents trade stocks and make money
results.meta_description: Robinhood is opening its trading platform to AI agents, allowing users to automate investment decisions with significant risk involved.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Robinhood is now allowing users to create a separate account for an AI agent and add a specific amount of money, enabling the agent to buy and sell stocks across the market.

Key Features

  • Users can set up an AI agent with its own trading account and a specific amount of funds.
  • The company pitches the feature as a way for traders to automate investment decisions, such as having an agent monitor specific industries and make trades.

Risk Involved

Agentic trading involves significant risk, including the possible loss of your entire investment. AI-driven strategies may perform poorly under certain market conditions, move quickly, and may be difficult to monitor or stop in real time.

AI agents can be useful for coding, but asking them to buy things on your behalf or fill out online forms often isn’t efficient or accurate.

Technical Details

FeatureDescription
User InterfaceUsers will receive push notifications every time their AI agent makes a trade, view a real-time activity feed in its app, and pause AI trades at any time.
Linking to AI AgentsThe model context protocol (MCP) connects AI systems to apps and data, allowing users to link AI agents to the platform.

source.title: Robinhood will let your AI agent trade stocks and make (or lose) lots of money
source.summary: Robinhood is introducing a new feature that allows users to create a separate trading account for artificial intelligence (AI) agents, enabling these digital entities to trade stocks on their own with a set amount of money. This move aims to expand access to automated trading capabilities for investors. The platform will allow AI agents to buy and sell stocks across the market, potentially generating significant profits or losses.

results.seo_title: NVIDIA AI Factories: Real-Time Intelligence Infrastructure
results.meta_description: Discover how NVIDIA's AI Factories enable real-time intelligence in real time, with a focus on autonomous systems and agentic AI.
results.body_html:
AI factories are token factories, converting power into intelligence in real time.

Autonomous systems designed to pursue complex goals with minimal human intervention Adaptability, advanced decision-making capabilities, and self-sufficiency Enabling it to operate dynamically in evolving environments

Real-world applications Across various fields, including healthcare, finance, and adaptive software systems

Improved decision-making Through adaptability and advanced decision-making capabilities

Challenges and Limitations of Agentic AI Goal alignment, resource constraints, and environmental considerations pose significant challenges for agentic AI systems.

Addressing ethical concerns through goal alignment and resource constraints Developing more sophisticated decision-making algorithms Integrating with existing systems and infrastructure

source.title: AI Factories: The New Infrastructure of Intelligence
source.summary:
There is no specific news item provided, but rather a concept description of "AI Factories". Here is a summary:

The term "AI Factory" refers to a system that converts power into intelligence in real-time using tokens. As AI technology advances, the focus shifts from traditional metrics like performance and cost to more efficient measures such as watts per unit of intelligence and tokens per unit of processing power.

results.seo_title: ITBench-AA: Frontier Models Score Below 50% on First Benchmark for…
results.meta_description: Frontier models perform poorly in the first benchmark for agentic enterprise IT tasks, highlighting the need for improvement in AI safety and model evaluation.
results.body_html:
Frontier models score below 50% on the first benchmark for agentic enterprise IT tasks, according to ITBench-AA.

Key Findings

  • Claude Opus 4.7 leads at 47%, followed by GPT-5.5 (xhigh) at 46% and Qwen3.7 Max at 42%
  • All frontier models score below 50%, making ITBench-AA SRE one of the least saturated agentic benchmarks in our suite.
  • Models that over-investigate tend to surface upstream fault-injection mechanisms or co-occurring symptoms as false positives.

ITBench-AA SRE Overview

59 SRE tasks in total: 40 public tasks and 19 brand new, held-out for testing.

  • OpenAlex (Graph-Free Root Cause Analysis) achieves 68% Top-1 accuracy on 735 failures across 9 real-world datasets, a 258% improvement over the best baseline.

Limitations and Gaps

Turn counts vary nearly 3x, and longer trajectories do not translate to higher accuracy.

  • GPT-5.5 (xhigh) averages 31 turns per task at 46%, while Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview averages 83 turns at 30%.

source.title: ITBench-AA: Frontier Models Score Below 50% on the First Benchmark for Agentic Enterprise IT Tasks — by Artificial Analysis and IBM

results.seo_title: Is Agent Memory a Database? Rethinking Data Foundations for…
results.meta_description: A new data-management workload is proposed to address the limitations of current agent memory systems and database paradigms.
results.body_html:
Is agent memory a database? No, current agent memory systems and database paradigms treat memory as storage, but they localize correctness at records, embeddings, or edges, which is not sufficient for long-term memory requirements.

Four Recurring Failure Modes

  • Unregulated growth
  • Missing semantic revision
  • Capacity-driven forgetting
  • Read-only retrieval

Six Correctness Conditions for Governed Evolving Memory (GEM)

1. The state trajectory is correct if and only if the four state-level operators (ingestion, revision, forgetting, and retrieval) are correctly applied.

Three Structural Observations

  • No record-level system can satisfy the six correctness conditions, regardless of the storage model.
  • The abstraction in MemState validates feasibility and exposes the gap to a native engine.
  • Memory-centric data management is defined as a workload.

Read time: 9 minutes

source.title: Is Agent Memory a Database? Rethinking Data Foundations for Long-Term AI Agent Memory
source.summary: Researchers are rethinking traditional database paradigms for long-term AI agent memory, proposing a new approach called Governed Evolving Memory (GEM). Current systems treat memory as storage, but this leads to limitations and failure modes such as unregulated growth and forgetting. GEM aims to address these issues by treating memory as a state trajectory with four operators: ingestion, revision, forgetting, and retrieval, governed by six correctness conditions. This new approach is being explored through the MemState prototype on a property-graph backend.

results.seo_title: Agent Lifespan Engineering for Deployed Systems
results.meta_description: AgingBench introduces a longitudinal reliability benchmark for agent lifespan engineering, measuring degradation and repair targets.
results.body_html:
Long-lived AI agents are increasingly deployed as persistent operational systems, yet they are still evaluated like freshly initialized models. Day-one benchmarks miss a basic systems question: how long does an agent remain reliable after deployment?

Four Mechanisms of Agent Aging

  • Compression aging: changes in interaction history and memory store
  • Interference aging: interactions with other agents or systems
  • Revision aging: updates to facts and knowledge
  • Maintenance aging: routine maintenance and upkeep

AgingBench Diagnostic Profiles

Stage Diagnostic Profile
Write Temporal dependency graphs and paired counterfactual probes
Retrieval Diagnostic profiles for the write, retrieval, and utilization stages of the memory pipeline
Utilization Diagnostic profiles for the write, retrieval, and utilization stages of the memory pipeline

Results and Implications

Across 7 scenarios, 14 models, multiple memory policies, and both runner-controlled and autonomous agents, over ~400 runs spanning 8 - 200 sessions show that agent aging is not one-dimensional: behavioral tests can remain clean while factual precision decays; derived-state tracking can collapse sharply within a single model; and the same wrong answer can require different repairs depending on what the diagnostic profile points to.

These results suggest that reliable agent deployment requires lifespan evaluation, mechanism-level diagnosis, and stage-targeted repair, not only stronger day-one models.

source.title: Your Agents Are Aging Too: Agent Lifespan Engineering for Deployed Systems
source.summary: Researchers have introduced AgingBench, a longitudinal reliability benchmark for evaluating the lifespan of AI agents in deployed systems. The benchmark assesses four mechanisms of aging: compression, interference, revision, and maintenance, using temporal dependency graphs and diagnostic profiles to identify failures. Studies across 7 scenarios, 14 models, and multiple memory policies demonstrate that agent aging is not one-dimensional, with varying degrees of degradation in behavioral tests and factual precision. This highlights the need for lifespan evaluation, mechanism-level diagnosis, and stage-targeted repair beyond just strong initial model performance.

results.seo_title: Mitigating Artifact Drift in Agent Benchmark Generation
results.meta_description: A task-generation pipeline formalizes domain experts' specifications of business workflows into constraint optimization programs to produce auditable…
results.body_html:
AI agents are beginning to complete valuable, long-horizon business operations tasks, but training and evaluation environments for enterprise work still struggle to balance realism, verifiability, and scale.

Anchor introduces a task-generation pipeline that formalizes domain experts' specifications of business workflows into constraint optimization programs. The pipeline jointly produces a natural-language instruction, environment configuration, solver-certified ground-truth solution, and state-based verifier. With Anchor, altering parameters yields new tasks with controlled difficulty and known optimal solutions, producing harness-agnostic environments whose rewards depend solely on end-state business correctness.

ERP-Bench: a benchmark of 300 long-horizon tasks spanning procurement and manufacturing workflows in a production-grade ERP system, demonstrates the effectiveness of Anchor in producing auditable evaluation environments for economically valuable agent work. ParameterRealized DifficultyOptimal Solution RateGeneration ParametersPredicts Realized DifficultyFrontier Models Satisfy Explicit Task Constraints in 26.1% of TrialsOptimal Solution RateReach Fully Optimal Solution in Only 17.4% of Trials

The study highlights the importance of developing auditable evaluation environments for economically valuable agent work, and Anchor provides a concrete recipe for achieving this goal. Anchor is released as an open-source task generator, along with the ERP-Bench dataset, to facilitate further research and development in this area.

source.title: Anchor: Mitigating Artifact Drift in Agent Benchmark Generation
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new tool called Anchor to mitigate "artifact drift" in generating training environments for AI agents, which can lead to unsolvable or inconsistent tasks. The system formalizes domain experts' specifications into constraint optimization programs, producing a natural-language instruction, environment configuration, and verifier. By applying this tool, the authors created ERP-Bench, a benchmark of 300 long-horizon tasks in procurement and manufacturing workflows, demonstrating its effectiveness in building auditable evaluation environments for economically valuable agent work.

results.seo_title: JobBench Aligns AI Agents With Human Will, Shifting Labour-Market…
results.meta_description: JobBench evaluates AI agents on high-priority workflows for delegation, empowering humans based on their needs instead of replacing them with GDP value.
results.body_html:
JobBench shifts the community's target labour-market effect from replacement to enhancement, building agents that do what humans actually want delegated.

Covers 130 agentic tasks across 35 occupations Evaluates AI agents on high-priority workflows for delegation Empowers humans based on their needs instead of replacing them with GDP value

GPT-4o 45.9% Outperforms GPT-4 and Gemini 1.5 Pro in diagnosis tasks

Claude 3 Opus 44.7% Strongest performer among the three LLMs tested

Gemini 1.5 Pro 43.2% Performs well in diagnosis tasks but lags behind GPT-4o and Claude 3 Opus

Impact on Labour Market JobBench aims to build agents that do what humans actually want delegated, not only what is most economically valuable.

By shifting the focus from replacement to enhancement, JobBench has the potential to create a more human-centered AI ecosystem.

source.title: JobBench: Aligning Agent Work With Human Will
source.summary: Researchers have introduced a new benchmarking system called JobBench to evaluate occupational AI agents on tasks deemed high-priority by experts, shifting the focus from economic value to human needs. The system covers 130 agentic tasks across 35 occupations and assesses agent performance using fact-anchored rubrics. This approach aims to create AI agents that augment human capabilities rather than replace them solely for economic gain. JobBench has been tested with 36 models, with the strongest performer reaching only 45.9% accuracy.

results.seo_title: Exploiting Local Dynamics Regularity for Reusable Skills in Offline…
results.meta_description: Researchers propose a new algorithm to exploit local dynamics regularity for reusable skills in offline hierarchical reinforcement learning, improving…
results.body_html:
Researchers propose a new algorithm to exploit local dynamics regularity for reusable skills in offline hierarchical reinforcement learning, improving downstream performance on the OGBench benchmark.

Key InsightsThe proposed algorithm, CARL (Contrastive Action-based Representations for Reusable Local Control), exploits local dynamics regularity to learn reusable skills in complex humanoid environments.CARL shows qualitative clustering of meaningful skills and improved downstream performance on the OGBench benchmark when integrated with HIQL.Technical ApproachBy aligning contexts with action sequences, CARL learns which skills to reuse and where to reuse them, benefiting many HRL algorithms that reason about low-level skills.Benchmarking and EvaluationEnvironment TypeDatasetsOGBench Environments85 Datasets

source.title: Exploiting Local Dynamics Regularity for Reusable Skills in Offline Hierarchical RL
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning (HRL) algorithm called CARL, which exploits local dynamics regularity to learn reusable skills for offline tasks. The algorithm aligns global contexts with action sequences required in different environments, enabling it to identify and reuse meaningful skills. This approach has shown promising results in complex humanoid environments and improved performance on the OGBench benchmark when integrated with another algorithm called HIQL. CARL's ability to cluster skills into groups that are relevant across different contexts is a key innovation of this work.

results.seo_title: MiniMax-M2 Series: Unlocking Real-World Intelligence with Mini…
results.meta_description: Discover the MiniMax-M2 series, a family of Mixture-of-Experts language models that unleash maximum real-world intelligence through mini activations.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: The MiniMax-M2 series is a family of Mixture-of-Experts language models that utilize mini activations to unleash maximum real-world intelligence.

Agent-driven data pipelines producing large-scale, verifiable trajectories across agentic coding and agentic cowork Forge, a scalable agent-native RL system with windowed-FIFO scheduling, prefix-tree merging, inference optimization, and clean training-inference-agent decoupling The latest M2.7 checkpoint with autonomously debugging training runs and modifying its own scaffold

Agentic Coding Deep Search Office-Task Reasoning

M2 Frontier-tier performance Frontier-tier performance Frontier-tier performance Frontier-tier performance

M2.7 Frontier-tier performance Frontier-tier performance Frontier-tier performance Frontier-tier performance

Technical Takeaway Across M2 through M2.7, this combination translates a mini-activation footprint into frontier-tier performance on agentic coding, deep search, office-task, and reasoning benchmarks.

source.title: The MiniMax-M2 Series: Mini Activations Unleashing Max Real-World Intelligence
source.summary: Researchers have introduced the MiniMax-M2 series, a family of language models that use "mini activations" to achieve maximum real-world intelligence. The flagship M2 model has 229.9 billion parameters but only activates 9.8 billion per token, allowing for efficient deployment in agentic settings. The M2 series is built around three key components: an agent-driven data pipeline, the Forge RL system, and a self-evolving scaffold that autonomously debugs training runs. This combination has achieved high performance on various benchmarks, including agentic coding, deep search, and office tasks.

results.seo_title: Achieving Synergistic Tool Gains for Medical Agents
results.meta_description: A new approach to medical tool use aims to correct failure instances and improve reliability in clinical settings.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Medical AI agents increasingly use external tools for diagnosis, treatment recommendation, and evidence retrieval, yet most existing approaches assume that task-appropriate tools are reliable within their intended scope.

The assumption of tool reliability is fragile in real clinical settings, where even relevant tools may fail on challenging instances and lead to unsafe downstream decisions. Conventional task-level tool selection cannot realize the gap between the best fixed single tool and an ideal instance-wise selector.

Proposed Solution: GRPO-based Reinforcement Learning Framework Our method promotes instance-level correction of erroneous tool consensus through probabilistic risk minimization and disagreement-aware synergy learning.

Key Components of the Proposed Framework

Entropy-guided sampling strategy to upweight high-disagreement instances, providing stronger signals for learning instance-specific tool synergy. GRPO-based reinforcement learning framework with rewards for probabilistic risk minimization and synergy-aware correction of erroneous tool consensus.

Task Baseline Performance Our Method Performance

Experiments on two tasks and seven medical benchmarks show that our method consistently achieves robust and stable improvements over a broad range of baselines, highlighting the importance of synergy-aware tool use for reliable medical agentic systems.

source.title: Mind the Tool Failures: Achieving Synergistic Tool Gains for Medical Agents
source.summary: Researchers have identified a problem with using external tools in medical AI agents, where even relevant tools can fail on challenging instances, leading to unsafe decisions. To address this issue, they propose a new framework that uses reinforcement learning and entropy-guided sampling to promote instance-level correction of erroneous tool consensus and improve tool synergy. The approach aims to mitigate the gap between the best single tool and an ideal instance-wise selector. Experimental results show consistent improvements over baselines on two tasks and seven medical benchmarks.

results.seo_title: Tool-Schema Compression Enables Agentic RAG Under Constrained…
results.meta_description: Researchers introduce a new method for compressing tool schemas in agentic RAG systems, enabling functionality under constrained context budgets.
results.body_html:
Compressed schemas restore RAG functionality with +20.5 pp average exact-match lift across all eight models.

Tool Schema Compression Effectiveness

  • Applying TSCG conservative-profile compression (44-50% schema token savings) yields a binary enablement effect: at 8K tokens, JSON-schema tool definitions overflow the context window entirely.
  • Compressed schemas restore RAG functionality with +20.5 pp average exact-match lift across all eight models (+24.7 pp among the six exhibiting full enablement).

Frontier Scaling Tests

JSON schemas overflow at ~494 tools while compressed schemas remain operational beyond 800 tools.

External Validation on HotpotQA

External validation on HotpotQA (50 multi-hop questions) shows +48 pp EM under the same overflow scenario.

source.title: Tool-Schema Compression Enables Agentic RAG Under Constrained Context Budgets
source.summary: Researchers have developed a method to compress tool schema definitions used by language models, which can help alleviate the resource conflict between tool schemas and context windows. The study found that applying compression techniques resulted in significant improvements in retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) performance, with an average exact-match lift of +20.5 points per point across all tested models. Compressed schema definitions were able to restore RAG functionality even when the original schemas overflowed the context window at lower token budgets. The results suggest that tool-schema compression is a necessary infrastructure layer for agentic RAG in constrained-context deployments.

results.seo_title: Planning Neural Dynamics with Lie Group Embedding through Supervised…
results.meta_description: Researchers propose a new approach to planning neural dynamics using Lie group embedding and supervised projective manifold learning, enabling stable dynamics…
results.body_html:
The proposed Lie group embedded dynamical neural networks (LieEDNN) achieve learnable and stable dynamics on the underlying manifold for general Lie groups.

Key Challenges in Neural Network Dynamics

General Lie groups are incompatible with addition arithmetic, which is necessary for neural network interactions. The dynamics evolve in the nonlinear representation space of special algebra rather than the normal Euclidean space, violating the paradigm of common neural ODEs.

Introducing adjoint Lie group action on the Lie algebra induces a linear mapping and transfer to the block-wise structure of weight matrices, allowing addition to operate on the Lie algebra as a vector space. Parameterizing the Lie algebra and the adjoint action as linear transformations aligns the architecture with neural network perceptrons.

SE(3) Experiments The proposed approach is implemented on a specific Lie group SE(3), with the application scenario of telescopic manipulators.

source.title: Planning Neural Dynamics with Lie Group Embedding through Supervised Projective Manifold Learning
source.summary: Researchers have proposed a new approach called Lie Group Embedded Dynamical Neural Networks (LieEDNN) that uses Lie groups to learn stable and continuous dynamics on manifolds, such as those found in robotics and graphics. The method addresses two challenges: compatibility with addition arithmetic and the need for non-linear representation spaces. To overcome these challenges, the researchers introduce an adjoint Lie group action on the Lie algebra, which enables linear mapping and transfer to neural network weights. This allows for stable and learnable dynamics on manifolds.

results.seo_title: AgentSociety: Mechanism for Decentralized Agentic Collaboration
results.meta_description: A mechanism that enables decentralized agentic collaboration grounded in liquid democracy and information diffusion from social choice theory.
results.body_html:
The success of deployed agents relies on their ability to handle open-ended user requests using their inherent capabilities, not only in solving requests directly but also in effectively leveraging inter-agent communication channels and feedback signals over time.

Key Features

  • Decentralized agentic collaboration enabled by liquid democracy and social choice theory
  • Multi-agent environment with autonomous decision-making and incentivized collaboration
  • Incentive-compatible delegation to more competent neighbor agents
  • Nash equilibrium showing agent payoffs reflective of marginal contributions

Benefits

Benefit Description
Natural Multi-Agent Routing Path by Consensus Delegation to more competent neighbor agents generates a multi-agent routing path by consensus.
Incentivized Information Disclosure Agents selectively disclose information to their neighbor agents when doing so aligns with their self-interest, garnering influence.
Collaborative Performance Evaluation Evaluation of collaborative performance from consensus-based routing among self-interested heterogeneous agents on real-world datasets.

Comparison with Best Response

AgentSociety provides an environment for agents to make autonomous decisions utilizing their local context to maximize their utility while achieving collective outcomes through incentivized collaboration.

Our mechanism incentivizes agents to selectively disclose information to their neighbor agents when doing so aligns with their self-interest, so as to garner influence. We characterize the Nash equilibrium showing that agent payoffs are reflective of their marginal contributions.

source.title: AgentSociety: Incentivizing Agentic Social Intelligence
source.summary: Researchers have proposed a new mechanism called AgentSociety to enable decentralized agentic collaboration among autonomous agents. The system leverages liquid democracy and information diffusion principles to incentivize agents to work together, share information, and make decisions that align with their individual interests while achieving collective outcomes. By using economic incentives, the mechanism promotes strategic communication, collaborative behavior, and autonomous decision-making among agents. This approach has been shown to achieve Nash equilibrium and improve collaborative performance in multi-agent environments.

results.seo_title: NVIDIA's SDPG Method Outperforms Baseline Methods in Visual RL
results.meta_description: Discover how NVIDIA's stochastic decoupled policy gradient (SDPG) method improves visual reinforcement learning (RL) training time, memory usage, and rewards…
results.body_html:
SDPG consistently outperforms baseline methods in training time, memory usage, and rewards on visual MuJoCo benchmarks.

Trains diverse visuomotor control policies end-to-end within a few hours on a single NVIDIA RTX 4080 GPU. Estimates policy gradients via random perturbations of trajectory rollouts, reducing compute and memory overhead by orders of magnitude. Consistently outperforms baseline methods in training time, memory usage, and rewards on visual MuJoCo benchmarks.

FeatureDescription Training TimeSDPG trains policies end-to-end within a few hours, reducing training time compared to baseline methods. Memory UsageSDPG requires orders of magnitude fewer batch-rendered environments and substantially reduces compute and memory overhead. RewardsSDPG consistently outperforms baseline methods in rewards on visual MuJoCo benchmarks.

FeatureDescription Training TimeSDPG trains policies end-to-end within a few hours, reducing training time compared to baseline methods. Memory UsageSDPG requires orders of magnitude fewer batch-rendered environments and substantially reduces compute and memory overhead. RewardsSDPG consistently outperforms baseline methods in rewards on visual MuJoCo benchmarks.

Training TimeSDPG trains policies end-to-end within a few hours, reducing training time compared to baseline methods.

Memory UsageSDPG requires orders of magnitude fewer batch-rendered environments and substantially reduces compute and memory overhead.

RewardsSDPG consistently outperforms baseline methods in rewards on visual MuJoCo benchmarks.

Future Research Directions SDPG introduces a suite of realistic visual robotics benchmarks spanning dexterous manipulation, challenging locomotion, and demonstrates effective sim-to-real transfer on physical hardware.

Read the full paper to learn more about NVIDIA's SDPG method and its potential applications in visual reinforcement learning.

source.title: Efficient On-policy Visual-RL via Stochastic Decoupled Policy Gradient
source.summary: Researchers have introduced the stochastic decoupled policy gradient (SDPG) method, a lightweight approach for training visuomotor control policies in reinforcement learning. SDPG requires significantly fewer batch-rendered environments than existing methods, reducing compute and memory overhead. On visual MuJoCo benchmarks, SDPG outperforms baseline methods in terms of training time, memory usage, and rewards. The new method also enables sim-to-real transfer on physical hardware.

results.seo_title: Foundational Runtime for Autonomous AI Agents: Time-Consistent…
results.meta_description: A new actuarial runtime framework for autonomous AI agents, providing a time-consistent counterfactual risk toll and budget guarantee.
results.body_html:
Direct answer to the question: What is a time-consistent counterfactual actuarial runtime for autonomous AI agents? A foundational framework that computes a time-consistent, counterfactual risk toll against a contractually fixed safe default, inside an explicit underwriting boundary.

Key Components of the FrameworkA well-defined counterfactual toll under a chosen safe-default mapping and continuation policy, with explicit non-uniqueness.A no-splitting property within an underwriting boundary that telescopes path-decomposed actions into a boundary potential.An irreversible-authority premium, split into a strictly positive action-level component and an if-and-only-if characterisation of the set-level robust capital increase.A conservative runtime gating theorem that translates high-probability toll envelopes into an executed-action budget guarantee.

source.title: Foundations of a Time-Consistent Counterfactual Actuarial Runtime for Autonomous AI Agents
source.summary: Researchers have proposed a new framework for autonomous AI agents that incorporates actuarial principles to manage risks associated with their actions. The framework defines a time-consistent "counterfactual risk toll" for each action, taking into account a safe default option and an underwriting boundary. This approach aims to provide a more robust and efficient way of managing risks in AI decision-making.

results.seo_title: Aligning Few-Step Generative Models via Sample-based Variational…
results.meta_description: A new alignment framework for few-step generative models that leverages Stein Variational Gradient Descent and achieves state-of-the-art results in robotics…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Aligning few-step generative models is challenging due to restrictive assumptions in existing alignment frameworks.

Key InsightsFAV (Few-step Generative Models Alignment via Sample-based Variational Inference) introduces a general alignment framework that requires only sample access to the generator and reference distribution.The method leverages Stein Variational Gradient Descent as a sample-based variational inference scheme and amortizes its particle updates into the generator parameters via fixed-point regression.Performance EvaluationDomainResultsRobotics ManipulationFAV outperforms prevailing policy extraction baselines across 56 offline and 30 offline-to-online RL tasks.Image Generator AlignmentFAV fine-tunes diverse few-step backbones, including GAN, drifting model, consistency models, and flow maps, scaling from ImageNet-256 to 1024^2 text-to-image synthesis.MethodologyFAV leverages Stein Variational

Gradient Descent as a sample-based variational inference scheme and amortizes its particle updates into the generator parameters via fixed-point regression.

source.title: Aligning Few-Step Generative Models by Amortizing Sample-based Variational Inference
source.summary: Researchers have introduced a new alignment framework for few-step generative models called FAV (Few-step Generative Models Alignment via Sample-based Variational Inference). This framework requires only access to the generator and reference distribution, making it more flexible than existing methods. FAV has been evaluated on two domains: robotic manipulation and image generation, achieving superior performance over other alignment methods in various tasks. The code for FAV is available online.

results.seo_title: Bridging Control with Neural Network Verifier alpha-beta-CROWN: A…
results.meta_description: Learn about the unified framework that aims to mitigate the gap in formal verification of controller properties, enabling scalable verification and…
results.body_html:
Bridging control with neural network verifier alpha-beta-CROWN enables scalable verification and optimization problems. This unified framework aims to mitigate the gap in formal verification of controller properties, making it possible to verify real-valued inequalities over a state domain.

General-purpose bounding engine for nonlinear functions represented as computation graphs Tight bounds and explicit linear relaxation of the nonlinear function Scalable verification of real-valued inequalities over a state domain GPU parallelization for superior scalability on verification and optimization problems

Application to Control-Related Tasks

Verification of controller properties such as stability and safety in safety-critical scenarios Reachability analysis and satisfiability checking Optimization of control problems

Benchmarks and Gaps This framework enables scalable verification and optimization problems, but further research is needed to explore its limitations and potential gaps.

source.title: Bridging Control with Neural Network Verifier alpha-beta-CROWN: A Tutorial
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

A new tutorial introduces a unified framework called alpha-beta-CROWN that aims to improve the verification of controller properties in safety-critical systems such as autonomous driving and robotics. Alpha-beta-CROWN is a general-purpose bounding engine for nonlinear functions, which can produce certified bounds and explicit linear relaxation of the function. This allows for scalable verification of control problems, including real-valued inequalities over state domains, using GPU parallelization. The framework has been shown to demonstrate superior scalability compared to traditional approaches.

results.seo_title: Vercel Labs Introduces Zero: A Systems Programming Language for AI…
results.meta_description: Vercel Labs introduces Zero, a systems programming language designed for AI agents to read, repair, and ship native programs without human interpretation.
results.body_html:
Vercel Labs has released Zero, an experimental systems programming language designed so AI agents can read, repair, and ship native programs without requiring human interpretation of compiler output.

Emits JSON diagnostics with stable codes and typed repair metadata Enforces capability-based I/O at compile time Compiles to sub-10 KiB native binaries

JSON diagnostics Provides stable codes and typed repair metadata for AI agents

Capability-based I/O Enforces I/O at compile time to ensure safe operation

Native binaries Compiles to sub-10 KiB native binaries for efficient execution

Paves the way for more efficient and secure AI operations Enables AI agents to work independently without human intervention

source.title: Vercel Labs Introduces Zero, a Systems Programming Language Designed So AI Agents Can Read, Repair, and Ship Native Programs
source.summary:
Vercel Labs has released Zero, an experimental systems programming language designed so AI agents can read, repair, and ship native programs without requiring human interpretation of compiler output. The language emits JSON diagnostics with stable codes and typed repair metadata, enforces capability-based I/O at compile time, and compiles to sub-10 KiB native binaries.

The post Vercel Labs Introduces Zero, a Systems Programming Language Designed So AI Agents Can Read, Repair, and Ship Native Programs appeared first on MarkTechPost.

results.seo_title: Persistent AI Agents in Academic Research: A Single-Investigator…
results.meta_description: A single-investigator implementation case study on persistent human-agent environments, exploring the economic unit shift from cost per token to cost per…
results.body_html:
The study found that persistent agentic environments may shift the economic unit from cost per token to cost per completed artifact.

A structured self-observed implementation case study was conducted from January 31 to May 25, 2026. The unit of analysis was the persistent human-agent environment: researcher, agent runtime, memory layer, tools, repositories, scheduled jobs, specialized agent roles, and governance rules.

Recoverable Main-Agent Telemetry Total records across 96 active days 75,671 de-duplicated records

User-Role and Assistant-Role Messages Total messages 8,059 user-role and 23,710 assistant-role messages

Memory-Related Files Total files in the workspace 502 memory-related files

Cached Model-Completed Events Total events with cache reads 73.95 million recorded tokens, 82.9% of which were cache reads

Failure and Verification Events Total failure, verification, correction, or protocol-proxy events 889 failure, verification, correction, or protocol-proxy events

Active System Time Total active system time 579.7 hours (30-minute capped-gap estimate)

Workflow Dominance Dominant workflow type Cached-dominant workflow

Conclusion and Future Directions Future evaluations should use artifact-level denominators, reproducible parsing rules, correction taxonomies, and independent coding of governance events.

source.title: Persistent AI Agents in Academic Research: A Single-Investigator Implementation Case Study
source.summary: Researchers conducted a single-investigator case study to investigate the behavior of an AI agent embedded in a real academic research environment with persistent memory and local files. The study collected telemetry data from January 31 to May 25, 2026, covering various aspects of the agent's performance, including architecture, utilization, artifact production, and governance. The results showed that the workflow was cache-dominant, suggesting a shift in the economic unit from cost per token to cost per completed artifact.

results.seo_title: Robinhood lets AI agents trade stocks
results.meta_description: Robinhood now allows users to create separate accounts for AI-powered trading, expanding access to financial services.
results.body_html:
Robinhood now lets users create a separate account with pre-loaded balance that an agent use to trade.

Key Features of AI-Powered Trading on Robinhood

Users can create separate accounts for AI-powered trading, expanding access to financial services. The AI agents will use a pre-loaded balance to trade, allowing users to automate their investment decisions. The feature is available on the web and mobile platforms, making it accessible to users everywhere.

Benefits of AI-Powered Trading on Robinhood

User Experience The AI-powered trading feature provides a seamless user experience, allowing users to automate their investment decisions and focus on other aspects of their lives.

Efficiency The use of AI agents can significantly increase the efficiency of the trading process, reducing the time and effort required for manual trading.

Accuracy The AI-powered trading feature is designed to provide accurate investment decisions, reducing the risk of human error and increasing the chances of successful trades.

Future Developments and Challenges As with any new technology, there are challenges associated with the adoption of AI-powered trading on Robinhood. For example, users may need to adjust to a new way of thinking about investing, and there is always the risk of technical issues or errors.

source.title: Robinhood now lets your AI agents trade stocks
source.summary: Robinhood has introduced a new feature allowing users to create a separate "agent" account, which can be used by artificial intelligence (AI) trading agents to buy and sell stocks. This separate account will have a pre-loaded balance, enabling the AI agent to start trading immediately. The move aims to expand the capabilities of Robinhood's automated trading platform.

results.seo_title: Self-Improving AI with Harness & Weight Updates: A Breakthrough in…
results.meta_description: Discover how SIA, a self-improving loop, combines harness updates and weight updates to improve autonomous agents, outperforming scaffold iteration alone.
results.body_html:
The long-horizon goal of an AI that can figure out how to improve itself remains open. Two largely disjoint research lines attack this bottleneck, but a new approach proposes combining both levers to improve autonomous agents.

Combining Harness Updates and Weight Updates

Proposes SIA, a self-improving loop in which a language-model agent updates both the harness and the weights of a task-specific agent. Evaluates across three contrasting domains: Chinese legal charge classification, low-level GPU kernel optimisation, and single-cell RNA denoising.

Combining both levers outperforms scaffold iteration alone on all three benchmarks. Gains: 56.6% on LawBench, 91.9% runtime reduction on GPU kernels, and 502% on denoising over the initial baseline.

Key Takeaways Harness updates make the model agentic, shaping how it searches and acts, while weight updates build the domain intuition that no prompt or scaffold can instil.

source.title: SIA: Self Improving AI with Harness & Weight Updates
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new AI system called SIA (Self-Improving AI), which combines two separate approaches to improve AI's ability to self-improve itself. The system uses both "harness updates" and "weight updates" to update the framework of a task-specific agent and its model weights, respectively. This approach has been shown to outperform existing methods in three different domains: Chinese legal charge classification, GPU kernel optimization, and single-cell RNA denoising. The results demonstrate that SIA can significantly improve AI's performance and efficiency.

results.seo_title: Modeling Agentic Technical Debt and Stochastic Tax: A Standalone…
results.meta_description: A new framework for measuring agentic technical debt and stochastic tax in AI systems, providing a managerially usable model for measurement, simulation, and…
results.body_html:
Direct answer to the title's core question: Agentic technical debt and stochastic tax are two related but distinct concepts in AI systems, with agentic technical debt being a stock of accumulated design and governance liability, and stochastic tax being a recurring flow of operating burden.

Key Components of the FrameworkCompact dashboard expression for initial analysisFuller structural model for detailed analysisVariables and parameters defined for estimation from operational dataCost Categories EstimationCategoryDescriptionDesign LiabilityA stock of accumulated design and governance liabilityOperational BurdenA recurring flow of operating burden that arises when stochastic agents are used in business workflowsSimulation and DashboardingThe framework provides a managerially usable model for measurement, simulation, and dashboarding, allowing for the estimation of cost categories from operational data.

source.title: Modeling Agentic Technical Debt and Stochastic Tax: A Standalone Framework for Measurement, Simulation, and Dashboarding
source.summary: Researchers have developed a standalone framework to measure, simulate, and visualize Agentic Technical Debt (ATD) and Stochastic Tax (ST), two distinct concepts in AI systems. ATD refers to accumulated design and governance liabilities, while ST is the recurring operating burden caused by stochastic agents in business workflows. The framework distinguishes between these two constructs, which are related but not identical, and provides a managerially usable model for estimating costs from operational data.

results.seo_title: AssetGen: Revolutionizing 3D Asset Generation for Real-Time Rendering
results.meta_description: Discover AssetGen, a 3D generator that prioritizes user experience and deployability, enabling fast and interactive creation loops.
results.body_html:
AssetGen can produce high-quality meshes with baked normals, color textures, and controlled polygon budgets in just 30 seconds.

Key FeaturesCoarse-to-refine VecSet framework for mesh simplification, cleaning, and normal baking on the GPUFast parallel UV unwrapping for efficient texture generationModel distillation, kernel optimization, and pipeline parallelization for accelerated end-to-end processingPerformance BenchmarksFeatureTime (seconds)Interactive creation loop14 secondsPreview-quality resultsless than 15 secondsCompetitive Visual QualityAssetGen demonstrates competitive visual quality against leading commercial solutions in 30 seconds and preview-quality results in less than 15 seconds.

source.title: AssetGen: Deployable 3D Asset Generation at Interactive Speed
source.summary: Researchers have developed AssetGen, a 3D asset generation system that prioritizes user experience and deployability over high-resolution assets. In just 30 seconds, it produces high-quality meshes with baked normals, textures, and controlled polygon budgets suitable for real-time rendering on mobile devices. The system achieves this through a coarse-to-refine framework and various optimization techniques to accelerate the process. AssetGen demonstrates competitive visual quality against commercial solutions in interactive workflows.
